name: Thomas Rhett
bio: Genre-blending country-pop-R&B. Autobiographical storytelling across a broad range: nostalgia, biographical life chapters, attraction, devotion, self-reflection. Where Russell Dickerson writes almost exclusively about his wife, Thomas Rhett writes across a much broader emotional range.
producer_notes: Known for genre-smashing productions that co-exist better than they have any right to. Collaborated with a wide range of producers. Hyper-specific lyric details — exact dates, drinks, songs, clothing — are the Rhett signature.

songs.title: After All the Bars Are Closed
songs.dna: Tried a range of styles including 'extremely Western, like if Midland had a baby.' Final version has live drum feel from drummer Jerry Roe, pedal steel waterfall intro. Rhett describes it as something that 'teleports you and makes you feel nostalgic, but also want to dance and want to be with the person you love.'
songs.style_prompt: Nostalgic modern country-pop, 118 BPM, live drummer with human-feel snare, pedal steel waterfall intro, electric guitar pickup-driven riff, warm dancing-and-longing duality, male vocal smooth and narrative, chorus phrases building on off-beat pickups for rhythmic anticipation, modern country production with Western vintage undertone, closing-time atmosphere, intimate yet anthemic
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a nostalgic country-pop song about a man who meets a woman at a bar near closing time and the story of where that night leads — not just a pickup, but the beginning of something real. The tone makes you want to dance and ache at the same time. The song should feel like a memory recalled from years later, so there's a golden-haze nostalgia over everything. The bridge should fast-forward: years later, he realizes that night changed everything. Under 3:30.

songs.title: Look What God Gave Her
songs.dna: Disguises a deep ballad about gratitude and love inside a bubbly, country-pop tune with one of the catchiest hooks in his catalog. Double-platinum single. Bright, upbeat, almost effortlessly catchy.
songs.style_prompt: Bubbly bright country-pop, 120 BPM, bouncy guitar-driven groove, punchy drum machine, male vocal charming and warm — utterly delighted, irresistible singalong chorus, deep gratitude disguised inside effortless production, layered harmonies, polished Nashville sheen, sounds easy but hits deep, hooks-first structure, joyful awe emotional core
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a bubbly, irresistible country-pop song where a man expresses awe at the woman he loves — cataloguing everything about her with joyful disbelief, and crediting something bigger than himself for putting her in his path. The tone is charming and completely besotted. The chorus is instantly singable. Verses are specific and observational — the way she moves, her voice, the way she doesn't even know how remarkable she is. Under 3:15.

songs.title: Life Changes
songs.dna: Anchored by affected electric guitar, methodical piano chords, and drum machine — eventually real drums, a steel-guitar-sounding instrument, and a horn set. The I-iii-vi-IV chord structure is unusual. The narrator stands in the background while still being the center of attention — his wife and kids get the spotlight.
songs.style_prompt: Upbeat genre-mishmash country-pop, 88 BPM, affected electric guitar, methodical piano chords, drum machine transitioning to live drums, subtle horn accents, unexpected minor chords under bright upbeat production, male vocal earnest and slightly surprised at his own life, autobiographical storytelling structure, each verse a new chapter of life, bouncy feel despite emotional weight
songs.lyric_prompt: Write an autobiographical country-pop song structured as a series of life chapters — each verse is a different age or era: being 18 and clueless, meeting the woman who changed everything, getting married, becoming a father. The tone is genuine surprise and gratitude. The key is to make it relatable: he's not bragging, he's sharing. The chorus is a joyful declaration that life keeps surprising him. Pop structure with country warmth. Under 3:15.

songs.title: Unforgettable
songs.dna: Starting with the exact date (October 14), Rhett recalls small-but-sweet details like the mango-rita drink and doing the Running Man dance. The specific details were completely made up — they just felt true. Smooth, breezy, R&B-influenced country-pop.
songs.style_prompt: Smooth breezy country-pop with R&B influence, 120 BPM, warm groove-driven production, male vocal silky and charming, hyper-specific lyrical detail — exact dates, drinks, dances, clothes, songs, layered harmonies on chorus, polished Nashville production with pop sheen, sounds like a perfect summer night recalled from years later, golden memory atmosphere
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a smooth, breezy country-pop song about a man recalling a specific unforgettable night when he met a woman — with hyper-specific details: the exact date, what she was drinking, the song that was playing, what she was wearing down to her shoes, a specific dance or moment. The specificity is what makes it universal. The chorus expands outward from those specific details into the bigger emotional statement. Include a pop culture or music reference naturally woven in. Under 3:20.

songs.title: Crash and Burn
songs.vibe: Doo-wop meets modern country-pop · tambourine banger
songs.dna: Smashing together country specialties with modern pop and retro doo-wop sounds. Notable tambourine-fueled pop banger. The central tension: two people who are wrong for each other but can't help choosing each other anyway.
songs.style_prompt: Retro doo-wop meets modern country-pop, 138 BPM, tambourine-forward percussion, bouncy rhythm, male vocal charming and knowing — a man who knows this is a bad idea and does it anyway, bright upbeat production masking complicated emotional content, retro pop production elements over Nashville country base, singalong anthemic chorus, hand claps, warm vintage warmth with modern polish
songs.lyric_prompt: Write an upbeat, bouncy country-pop song about a man in a relationship that he knows is probably going to end badly — and he's choosing to enjoy the ride anyway. He can see the crash coming and jumps in anyway because she's worth it. The production should feel retro-pop-fun. The chorus is an irresistible, singalong declaration of choosing chaos. Under 3:20.

songs.title: Us Someday
songs.dna: A more tender, hopeful track — projecting forward into a future together. Where most Rhett songs look back or celebrate the present, this one looks ahead. The production is warm and slightly more restrained, acoustic-leaning.
songs.style_prompt: Warm acoustic country-pop ballad, 85 BPM, acoustic guitar and piano blend, pedal steel tenderness, male vocal sincere and hopeful — projecting forward into the future, gentle drum groove, layered harmonies building on chorus, more restrained than Rhett's uptempo work, emotionally open and forward-looking, future-tense romantic imagery
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a tender country-pop ballad from a man imagining the future he wants to build with the woman he loves — not looking back, not celebrating now, but projecting forward. He sees their kids growing up, the front porch they'll eventually have, the decades ahead of them, growing old together. He's describing exactly what that future looks like in small, real details. The bridge should be a moment of quiet awe at the scope of what they're building. Under 3:45.
